name: ccxt
description: CCXT cryptocurrency trading library. Use for cryptocurrency exchange APIs, trading, market data, order management, and crypto trading automation across 150+ exchanges. Supports JavaScript/Python/PHP.
capabilities: Explain CCXT library architecture and exchange integration patterns
Provide code examples for fetching market data from 150+ exchanges
Help with order placement, management, and cancellation operations
Guide implementation of WebSocket streaming with CCXT Pro
Assist with authentication, API keys, and exchange credentials
Troubleshoot common issues and error handling scenarios
author: 2025Emma
securitySummary: This skill contains only static markdown documentation extracted from official CCXT sources. No executable code, scripts, network calls, or file system access capabilities are present. All 328 static findings are false positives triggered by documentation content patterns (URLs, code examples, API references) rather than actual security risks.
valueStatement: CCXT is a complex library supporting 150+ cryptocurrency exchanges. This skill provides comprehensive documentation and examples for implementing trading APIs, market data retrieval, and automated trading strategies across multiple exchanges.
userTitle: Get help with CCXT cryptocurrency trading library
limitations: Does not execute trades or access user exchange accounts directly
Does not provide real-time market data or price feeds
Cannot install or configure the CCXT library on user systems
Reference documentation is static and may not reflect latest exchange changes

useCases.title: Build trading bots
useCases.description: Create automated trading strategies that work across multiple cryptocurrency exchanges with unified API calls

useCases.title: Connect to exchanges
useCases.description: Integrate cryptocurrency exchange APIs into trading platforms, dashboards, or financial applications

useCases.title: Access market data
useCases.description: Fetch prices, order books, trades, and OHLCV data from 150+ exchanges for analysis or display

promptTemplates.title: Basic usage
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I install and set up CCXT in Python to connect to Binance and fetch the current BTC/USDT price?

promptTemplates.title: Trading operations
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to create a limit buy order and a market sell order using CCXT. Include error handling.

promptTemplates.title: WebSocket streaming
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I use CCXT Pro to watch real-time order book updates via WebSocket? Provide a complete async example.

promptTemplates.title: Multi-exchange
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I fetch ticker data from multiple exchanges simultaneously and compare prices across them for arbitrage opportunities?

bestPractices: Always enable rate limiting to avoid exchange API bans: exchange.enableRateLimit = true
Reuse exchange instances throughout your application instead of creating new ones
Load markets once with loadMarkets() before trading to improve performance
Never share API keys or secrets in code examples or support requests
antiPatterns: Creating new exchange instances for each API call (resets rate limiter)
Ignoring rate limits and getting banned from exchange APIs
Forgetting to handle API errors and exceptions in production code
Hardcoding API keys in source code instead of using environment variables

faq.question: Which programming languages does CCXT support?
faq.answer: CCXT supports JavaScript, Python, PHP, and TypeScript. The library works in Node.js and browsers for JavaScript, asyncio for Python, and ReactPHP for PHP.

faq.question: How many exchanges does CCXT support?
faq.answer: CCXT supports over 150 cryptocurrency exchanges. Use exchange.id to get the full list of supported exchange identifiers programmatically.

faq.question: How do I switch to sandbox mode for testing?
faq.answer: Call exchange.setSandboxMode(true) immediately after creating the exchange instance. Sandbox APIs use the same interface but with virtual test funds.

faq.question: Is my API key data safe with CCXT?
faq.answer: CCXT runs locally on your machine. Never share your keys in public forums. Use environment variables or secure key management for credentials.

faq.question: Why am I getting rate limit errors?
faq.answer: Enable the built-in rate limiter with exchange.enableRateLimit = true. Reuse exchange instances and avoid creating new ones frequently.

faq.question: What is the difference between CCXT and CCXT Pro?
faq.answer: CCXT provides REST API access for trading and market data. CCXT Pro adds WebSocket streaming for real-time updates on prices, orders, and trades.
